subject: move docker-build groovy script to docker repo
commit_message:
move docker-build groovy script to docker repo

For all docker builds, policy is using groovy-maven-plugin
to run a groovy script to determine the tag for docker.

The plan is:
1, move docker-tag groovy script to docker repo.
2, change source for groovy-maven-plugin to a URL
   below, that will point to the source code from
   step #1.

   <configuration>
       <source>https://raw.githubusercontent.com/onap
       /policy-docker/master/utils/groovy
       /docker-tag.groovy</source>
   </configuration>

Issue-ID: POLICY-3378
Change-Id: Iff1f09e5ae47205240305b09602c49d403fad98f
Signed-off-by: Taka Cho <takamune.cho@att.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-docker-master-verify-java/278/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/policy-docker-master-verify-java/278

Patch Set 1: -Code-Review

should instead of the directory "src/main/groovy" in the root of policy/docker be under some sort of directory for example "groovy" or "scripts" or "utilities" and then put the file under there directly?  ie. "utilities/docker-tag.groovy" instead of "src/main/groovy/docker-tag.groovy" ?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: -Code-Review
> 
> should instead of the directory "src/main/groovy" in the root of policy/docker be under some sort of directory for example "groovy" or "scripts" or "utilities" and then put the file under there directly?  ie. "utilities/docker-tag.groovy" instead of "src/main/groovy/docker-tag.groovy" ?

I think I am following the maven project standard file structure for groovy. groovy scripts should be under src/main/groovy.

Patch Set 1:

> 
> I think I am following the maven project standard file structure for groovy. groovy scripts should be under src/main/groovy.

right, but without a project name and without pom file.  My issue is when I clone policy/docker, and I do a "ls -l" on it I will see:

Mode	Name	Size	
-rw-r--r--	.env	23	logstatsplain
-rw-r--r--	.gitignore	71	logstatsplain
-rw-r--r--	.gitreview	88	logstatsplain
-rw-r--r--	INFO.yaml	2181	logstatsplain
-rw-r--r--	LICENSE.txt	596	logstatsplain
-rw-r--r--	README.md	666	logstatsplain
d---------	csit	1007	logstatsplain
d---------	helm 	50	logstatsplain
d---------	policy-db-migrator	65	logstatsplain
d---------	policy-jdk	68	logstatsplain
d---------	policy-jre	68	logstatsplain
-rw-r--r--	pom.xml	1951	logstatsplain
d---------	releases	336	logstatsplain
d---------	src             336	logstatsplain                   **** <-- here
-rw-r--r--	version.properties	391	logstatsplain

the src/ there at the top level doesn't seem to combine well with the current repo layout which seems to be organized under different project names identifying the theme of what goes underneath.

In any case what do others think about the resulting layout?
